steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.Iovem: 'Iovem' is a proper noun in the accusative meaning 'Jupiter'
hints.Statorem: 'Statorem' is a title/epithet in the accusative meaning 'the Stayer (defender)'
hints.custodem: 'custodem' is a noun in the accusative meaning 'guardian'

hints.to summon to court: 'to summon' is 'citare' — 'invocare' means 'to call upon (a god)'

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'invocemus' — 'invocamus' is indicative

hints.voco, vocare, vocavi, vocatum: These are the parts of 'vocare' (to call) — the base verb
hints.provoco, provocare, provocavi, provocatu: These are the parts of 'provocare' (to call forth/challenge)
hints.revoco, revocare, revocavi, revocatum: These are the parts of 'revocare' (to call back)

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Iuppiter' — 'Iovem' is the accusative of this irregular noun
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'Iovi' — 'Iovem' is accusative, object of 'invocamus'

hints.1st: 1st declension accusative ends in -am — 'Iuppiter, Iovis' is an irregular 3rd declension noun
hints.2nd: 2nd declension accusative ends in -um — 'Iuppiter, Iovis' is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension accusative ends in -um — 'Iuppiter, Iovis' is 3rd declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'custos' — 'custodem' is accusative, in apposition with 'Iovem'
Nominative would be 'urbs' — 'urbis' with -is ending is genitive
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'urbi' — 'urbis' is genitive, modifying 'custodem'

steps.target: Jupiter in his role as 'the one who halts' (enemies) — whose temple stood nearby
choices: Jupiter as king of the gods
Jupiter in his role as 'the one who halts' (enemies) — whose temple stood nearby

hints.Jupiter as king of the gods: While Jupiter is king, 'Stator' specifically means 'the one who stops/stays' — referring to his role in halting enemies in battle
hints.A different deity named Stator: 'Stator' is an epithet of Jupiter, not a separate deity — from 'stare/sistere' (to stand/halt)
hints.Jupiter as god of storms: Jupiter governs storms, but 'Stator' specifically refers to halting the enemy, relevant to Catiline's threat

translation: We call upon Jupiter the Stayer, guardian of this city.
